trigger.stage: post-e16 matrix admission and label-independent e32 search diagnostics; before any enrichment metric, QUBO fit, validation sampling, or test release
trigger.finding: Five of seven flagged e16 pairs passed e32 rescue. L000348/2BAJ converged at e32 but differed from the e16 median by 2.16 kcal/mol, while L000348/3KQ7 retained one unfavorable alternate pose approximately 10.83 A from the other two e32 poses.
trigger.consequence: The unchanged e16 primary and sensitivity matrices remain rejected. No e32 diagnostic score may selectively replace an e16 matrix cell.

scope: Increase only the global Vina search strength from e16 to e32 and replace the overly sensitive max-minus-min gate with a preregistered two-of-three robust-consensus gate. Receptor pool, ligand panel, labels, box, seeds, preparation, scoring, CPU per job, and QUBO non-degeneracy requirements remain unchanged.

uniform_e32_recomputation.primary_aggregation: median representative score across three paired seeds
uniform_e32_recomputation.sensitivity_aggregation: minimum representative score across three paired seeds

e32_matrix_admission.consensus_definition: a replicate agrees when its score is no more than 0.5 kcal/mol above the minimum score for that receptor-ligand pair
e32_matrix_admission.raw_maximum_minus_minimum_range: reported as a sensitivity diagnostic but not a rejection criterion because one isolated unfavorable seed is tolerated by the frozen median aggregation
e32_matrix_admission.failure_action: stop before QUBO fitting; run label-independent e64 diagnostics on every failed consensus pair and do not selectively replace cells

post_admission_gate.action: run the unchanged train-only QUBO non-degeneracy gate from the original preregistration

interpretation_boundary: This amendment repairs search reliability using label-independent evidence. It cannot establish enrichment, receptor complementarity, biological activity, affinity accuracy, QUBO benefit, test performance, or quantum advantage.
